# Onboarded trusted voices: collective scenius narrative

**Status:** Working note (synthesis, not canonical doctrine)
**Created:** 2026-06-04
**Source corpus:** `docs/movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/`
**Desktop PDF:** `pnpm research:scenius-narrative:pdf` (writes HTML + PDF to the Desktop)
**Related doctrine:** [movement-leaders-as-ecosystem-layer.md](../strategy/movement-leaders-as-ecosystem-layer.md) · [homepage-ia-and-eeat-consultation-2026-05-12.md](./homepage-ia-and-eeat-consultation-2026-05-12.md)

---

## 1. What story are we telling?

When Movemental surfaces **trusted voices** on the public site, in credibility bands, or in a future network visualization, we are not listing "partners" or a fourth audience funnel. We are naming a **beginning scenius**—a field of practitioners whose bodies of work already overlap in real life, but whose **digital expression is scattered**.

Read together, the onboarded cohort in `_onboarded_leaders` tells one coherent story:

> A generation of North American (and globally connected) movement leaders has spent thirty years re-teaching the church how to **live sent**, **multiply**, and **organize at human scale**—often from the same conferences, imprints, and training networks—while each leader's sermons, books, cohorts, and podcasts still live on separate domains with weak cross-links.

Movemental's job is not to invent that network. It is to **make the existing network legible, linkable, and inspectable** without turning leaders into a recruiting roster.

---

## 2. Roster at a glance

Eighteen folders exist under `_onboarded_leaders`, but **three of them are the Chambers dyad** (`jeremy-chambers`, `monica-chambers`, `jeremy-monica-chambers`), which resolves to a single trusted voice. That yields **sixteen distinct voices**: fifteen research-complete movement leaders plus one unresolved identity (`bree-mills`, on hold).

| Slug | Primary public role | Signature contribution | Corpus confidence |

| [brad-brisco](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/brad-brisco/) | Send Network; Forge KC; Sentralized | Missional ecclesiology, co-vocational planting | High |
| [dave-ferguson](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/dave-ferguson/) | Exponential; NewThing; Community Christian Church | Hero-making, multiplication, *B.L.E.S.S.* | High |

| [hugh-halter](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/hugh-halter/) | Missio; MCAP; kingdom ecosystems | Incarnational community, bivo, "less churchy" mission | High |
| [rob-wegner](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/rob-wegner/) | KC Underground; Exponential Microchurch Next | Decentralized / starfish ecclesiology | High |
| [brian-sanders](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/brian-sanders/) | Underground Network (Tampa) | Microchurch operational model + story | High |
| [rowland-smith](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/rowland-smith/) | Forge America; Pando Collective | Forge USA operations, *Red Skies* curator | High |
| [jeremy-monica-chambers](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/jeremy-monica-chambers/) | **Dyad** — Forge America (Jeremy, Training Director); Arise Network; Pando Denver | Missional spirituality at the Renovaré × Forge seam; *The Art of Missional Spirituality*; 31 Sacred Practices | High |
| [peyton-jones](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/peyton-jones/) | NewBreed Training | *Church Plantology*; global planter training | High |
| [roy-moran](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/roy-moran/) | New Generations; Shoal Creek | **Hybrid Church**—DMM on-ramp for attractional pastors | High |
| [liz-rios](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/liz-rios/) | Passion2Plant; Púlpito Fellows | BIPOC woman-led planting; mujerista theology | High |
| [michael-cooper](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/michael-cooper/) | Ephesiology / TELOS | Missiological theology of movements; deep corpus | High |
| [meghan-good](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/meghan-good/) | AMBS DMin faculty | Scripture hermeneutics; movement narrative recovery | Medium–High |
| [andrew-jones](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/andrew-jones/) | Tall Skinny Kiwi | Global aid / emerging church digital pioneer | High (different lane) |
| [bree-mills](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/bree-mills/) | — | Identity not resolved in research pass | Low / hold |

> **Dyad note — Jeremy & Monica Paredes Chambers.** Treat the Chambers as **one trusted voice**, not two. Their books, Renovaré pieces, podcasts, and Forge/Arise roles are joint ("we" voice) unless explicitly noted; Monica (maiden name Paredes; Costa Rica–born) is co-author, not a footnote. The canonical folder is [`jeremy-monica-chambers`](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/jeremy-monica-chambers/) ([dyad substrate](../../movement_leader_research/_onboarded_leaders/jeremy-monica-chambers/CHAMBERS_DYAD_RESEARCH_COLLATED.md)); the per-person folders (`jeremy-chambers`, `monica-chambers`) are superseded and should collapse into a **single node** on `/voices` and in the graph. Wherever this note says "the Chambers," read the dyad.

---

## 3. The intellectual map (what they are collectively "about")

Six overlapping theme bands cover almost the entire cohort. No single leader owns a band alone; the scenius value is **coverage + cross-citation**.

### Band A — Apostolic imagination & fivefold ministry
**Anchors:** Alan Hirsch, JR Woodward (forewords, 100M publishing), Rowland Smith (Forge America), the Chambers (100M / Forge books).
**Ideas in play:** APEST, apostolic movements, "church with Jesus at the center," reframing leadership beyond clerical defaults.
**Audience:** Leaders who feel their tradition is structurally mis-shaped, not merely under-programmed.

### Band B — Multiplication at institutional scale
**Anchors:** Dave Ferguson, Peyton Jones (Exponential Series), Rob Wegner (NewThing / Exponential).
**Ideas in play:** Hero-makers, Level 5 churches, reproducible leadership pipelines, global planting networks.
**Audience:** Senior pastors and network leaders who already speak "multisite" and want **movement language without abandoning their church**.

### Band C — Microchurch & decentralized organization
**Anchors:** Brian Sanders, Rob Wegner, Rowland Smith (Pando), the Chambers (Arise RVA → Arise Network → Pando Denver).
**Ideas in play:** Microchurch as default unit, starfish structures (*The Starfish and the Spirit*), networks that serve mission rather than central programs.
**Audience:** Planters and network architects betting on **smaller, faster, place-based** ecclesiology.

### Band D — Incarnational presence & neighborhood mission
**Anchors:** Hugh Halter, Brad Brisco (+ Lance Ford), Dave Ferguson (*B.L.E.S.S.*).
**Ideas in play:** Tangible kingdom, "right here right now," co-vocational life, kingdom ecosystems (Halter's Lantern / Post Commons).
**Audience:** Practitioners tired of attractional transfer and ready to **embed in place**.

### Band E — Disciple-making movements & the attractional bridge
**Anchor:** Roy Moran (distinctive).
**Ideas in play:** Obedience-based DMM, *Hybrid Church* as on-ramp for Willow-era pastors, global data via New Generations.
**Audience:** Suburban senior leaders who will not join a post-evangelical missional seminar but **will** follow a peer who already converted a 1,000-seat church.

### Band F — Scholarship, formation, and global witness
**Anchors:** Michael Cooper (Ephesiology), Meghan Good (hermeneutics / narrative), Andrew Jones (global digital witness), Liz Rios (mujerista + institutional justice), the Chambers (Renovaré spiritual formation × mission).
**Ideas in play:** Missiology as theology, contemplative formation + mission (the Chambers' Renovaré × Forge seam), bilingual/BIPOC planting justice.
**Audience:** Seminaries, cohort operators, and leaders who need **rigor + pastoral voice**, not hype.

---

## 4. Network topology: hubs, bridges, and geography

### Structural hubs (high betweenness)

| Hub | Why it matters in this cohort |
|-----|------------------------------|
| **Alan Hirsch** | Co-authorship, forewords, Forge/100M imprint, academic orbit—default **theological gravity well**. |
| **Dave Ferguson** | Exponential + NewThing merger (2025); Zondervan Exponential Series; endorsement ring across megachurch leaders. |
| **Forge / 100Movements** | Shared training grammar: Brisco, Halter, Rowland, the Chambers, Woodward (publishing), Liz Rios (*Need to Know*). |
| **Exponential conference circuit** | Ferguson, Peyton Jones, Halter, Sanders (foreword), Wegner, Rowland—**operational** more than theological. |
| **Renovaré formation line** | The Chambers (Institute alumni + Ministry Team) bridge Foster/Willard spiritual-formation credibility into the missional cohort—a hub no one else in the roster touches. |
| **Fuller / seminary adjacency** | Woodward, Rowland, Liz Rios, Cooper (guest lectures)—credibility for **institutional** readers. |

### Bridge leaders (connect otherwise separate clusters)

| Bridge | Connects |
|--------|----------|
| **Rob Wegner** | Hirsch/Ford **starfish theology** ↔ Ferguson/NewThing **multiplication industrial complex**; KC Underground ↔ Underground Network family. |
| **Dave Ferguson** | Megachurch multiplication ↔ Hirsch (*On the Verge*). |
| **Roy Moran** | Dallas/Willow attractional world ↔ global DMM practitioners. |
| **JR Woodward** | Missio Alliance diversity board ↔ V3 planting ↔ Hirsch publishing. |
| **Liz Rios** | 100M / Alan orbit ↔ justice/sojourners/Fuller ↔ Passion2Plant **live cohorts**. |
| **The Chambers** | Renovaré contemplative line ↔ Forge missional line; Pando Collective ↔ Arise Network. |

### Geographic scenius nodes

- **Kansas City:** Brisco, Lance Ford (off-cohort but adjacent), Wegner, Moran—missional + microchurch + DMM in one metro.
- **Front Range (CO):** Rowland Smith, Pando Collective, the Chambers (Pando Denver, post-Richmond).
- **Tampa / Underground lineage:** Sanders; network edges to Wegner/Pulley (research notes).
- **Chicago:** Ferguson institutional base.
- **Denver / Adullam line:** Halter history; ongoing Forge/MCAP.

Geography is not branding—it is evidence that **relationships pre-exist the platform**.

---

## 5. Combined work: what they have already built together

Treating the cohort as a single "portfolio of practice" (without implying formal partnership):

| Collective asset | Evidence in corpus |
|------------------|-------------------|
| **Shared publishing imprints** | 100 Movements Publishing, IVP Forge/Shapevine lineage, Zondervan Exponential Series |
| **Co-authored & multi-author volumes** | *On the Verge* (Hirsch + Ferguson), *Starfish and the Spirit* (Hirsch + Ford + Wegner), *Red Skies* (Rowland + 10 voices incl. Sanders), *Need to Know* (Rios + Hirsch + others), *The Art of Missional Spirituality* (the Chambers, co-authored), Brisco/Ford catalog |
| **Training pipelines** | Forge America (Rowland; Jeremy Chambers as Training Director), Arise Network + Pando Denver (the Chambers), V3 (Woodward), NewBreed (Peyton Jones), Passion2Plant / Púlpito Fellows (Rios), MCAP/Missio (Halter), Send Network (Brisco) |
| **Conference knowledge commons** | Exponential, Verge, Sentralized, Mosaix—same stages, different emphases |
| **Endorsement graph** | Dense blurbs among Hirsch, Brisco, Ferguson, Woodward, Halter, Cooper, the Chambers—**social proof is already mutual** |

**What they have not built together (yet):** a unified, interlinked **public corpus** with consistent metadata, bi-directional citations, and inspectable "who taught what to whom" trails. That gap is the fragmentation story Movemental exists to address.

---

## 6. EEAT: why this group reads as authoritative

Google's EEAT frame maps cleanly onto *practitioner scenius*—not celebrity.

### Experience
- **Lived ecclesial experiments:** Underground (Sanders), KC Underground (Wegner), Adullam/Lantern (Halter), Arise RVA → Arise Network / Pando Denver (the Chambers), Shoal Creek hybrid journey (Moran), Community CCC (Ferguson).
- **Field planting:** Peyton Jones (three continents), Brisco (20+ years), Woodward (30+ years), the Chambers (movements reported in 40+ countries).
- **Operator-led cohorts:** Liz Rios (Passion2Plant, Lilly-funded Púlpito Fellows)—not only authors but **pipeline runners**.

### Expertise
- **Terminal degrees & research:** Woodward (PhD Manchester), Cooper (PhD TEDS, archaeology volume), Rowland (DMiss Fuller), Good (Duke/Portland), Rios (EdD, DMin, Union MA); the Chambers (Renovaré Institute alumni; TEDS / Lancaster Bible College).
- **Named frameworks:** APEST, Hero Maker, Hybrid Church, Church Plantology, Ephesiology, 31 Sacred Practices (the Chambers), microchurch definition (Sanders).
- **Institutional roles:** National directors (Rowland; Jeremy Chambers, Forge America Training Director; Halter; Woodward), Send/Exponential/New Generations leadership.

### Authoritativeness
- **Third-party validators:** Zondervan/IVP/Thomas Nelson/100M imprints; Exponential as largest North American planting conference; NAMB/Send; Renovaré Ministry Team; seminary adjunct lines; *Outreach* cover (Rios).
- **Cross-endorsement density:** Ferguson ↔ Stanley/Groeschel/Stetzer; Hirsch ↔ Frost/McNeal; Cooper ↔ Hirsch; the Chambers ↔ Hirsch / Foster orbit—**the graph is the authority**.

### Trustworthiness
- **Doctrinal transparency:** Each dossier preserves theological positioning without flattening disagreements (e.g., missional vs. 9Marks reviews on Brisco).
- **Non-recruiting stance:** Movemental doctrine explicitly rejects "apply to be featured" dynamics—trust increases when voices are **named for proof, not growth hacking**.
- **Consent-forward corpus:** Onboarding research is for **voice-faithful** future agents/courses, not scraping for SEO spam.

**EEAT packaging recommendation (public site):** Pair any scenius visualization with **inspectable edges**—book, foreword, org role, city—rather than a logo wall. Initials-as-decoration undermines the same trust EEAT needs (see homepage EEAT note).

---

## 7. Intersections and productive tensions

### Strong intersections (collaboration-ready)

| Intersection | Leaders | Shared deliverable shape |
|--------------|---------|---------------------------|
| Forge training curriculum | Rowland, the Chambers, Brisco, Halter | Modular cohort content, assessment rubrics |
| Microchurch playbooks | Sanders, Wegner, Rowland/Pando, the Chambers (Arise) | Course + field guide + network policy templates |
| Multiplication + missional grammar | Ferguson, Hirsch, Wegner | "Verge → ongoing" style co-teaching series |
| Seminary-aligned courses | Woodward, Rowland, Good, Cooper | DMin/MA modules with shared outcome language |
| BIPOC planting + justice | Rios, Woodward (Missio board), Halter | Cohort sponsorship, bilingual resources |
| DMM + missional | Moran, Cooper, Brisco | Pastors' bridge curriculum (obedience + sentness) |
| Formation + mission | The Chambers, Good, Halter | Contemplative-missional practicum (Renovaré × Forge) |

### Productive tensions (do not flatten)

| Tension | Why it matters |
|---------|----------------|
| **Movement theology vs. multiplication industrial complex** | Hirsch/Forge vs. Exponential metrics—Wegner and Ferguson are bridges; don't collapse into one voice. |
| **Microchurch as norm vs. one form among many** | Sanders/Wegner vs. the Chambers ("*a* form, not *the* form"). |
| **CPM/DMM jargon vs. Cooper's Ephesiology** | Cooper explicitly refuses operational CPM vocabulary—honor register. |
| **Contemplative vs. activist pace** | The Chambers / Halter vs. Ferguson / Peyton Jones event energy. |
| **Global digital witness vs. US planting networks** | Andrew Jones is adjacent, not central—prevents false "single movement" narrative. |

Naming tensions in copy **increases** trust; smoothing them reads as marketing.

---

## 8. How they could collaborate (concrete patterns)

These are **relationship-native** collaborations suggested by existing edges—not a mandate for new programs.

1. **Cross-linked "pathways" on platform**
   - Example: Ferguson *Hero Maker* ↔ Woodward *Scandal of Leadership* ↔ Moran *Hybrid Church* as a **leadership ethics + multiplication** trail.
   - Example: Sanders *Microchurches* ↔ Wegner *Starfish* ↔ Brisco co-vocational planting as a **scale-down + sustain** trail.

2. **Co-taught cohort seasons (Forge / Exponential adjacency)**
   - Rowland + the Chambers: contemplative missional practicum.
   - Brisco + Peyton Jones: planting methodology + Send/NAMB policy context.
   - Halter + Liz Rios: incarnational neighborhood + BIPOC church planting justice.

3. **Multi-author evergreen series**
   - Repeat *Red Skies* pattern: one curator (Rowland proved the model) + 6–8 voices per theme per year.
   - *Need to Know* pattern: anthology with **explicit** cross-profiles on Movemental.

4. **Foreword / endorsement swaps with platform attribution**
   - Already habitual in print; digitize with **persistent bi-directional links** (EEAT + discovery).

5. **Shared "fragmentation audits" as public proof**
   - Several leaders have `fragmentation-story.md` in corpus—collective publish would demonstrate Movemental's thesis with **named** inventories (books, PDFs, podcasts, LMS silos).

6. **Operator ↔ scholar pairs**
   - Cooper ↔ Moran (movement theology ↔ hybrid DMM practice).
   - Good ↔ the Chambers (Scripture narrative ↔ spiritual disciplines / Renovaré formation).

---

## 9. Linking strategy (technical + editorial)

### Editorial rules
- **Default public label:** "Trusted voices" (not Scenius™ in H1—doctrine).
- **Bi-directional:** If Alan profiles Brad, Brad's profile lists Alan with relationship type.
- **Typed edges:** `co-authored` > `foreword-for` > `same-org` > `endorsed` > `conference` (align with field-map weights in TAM research).
- **One node per voice:** The Chambers are a **single dyad node** (canonical slug `jeremy-monica-chambers`); never render Jeremy and Monica as two nodes. Apply the same rule to any future couple / co-author unit.
- **No false completeness:** Only onboarded leaders with dossiers appear as **nodes**; mention off-cohort collaborators (Frost, Ford, Smay) as **external** references, not implied tenants.

### Suggested link types on Movemental surfaces

| Surface | Link behavior |
|---------|----------------|
| `/voices` | Card → leader profile; optional "related voices" from graph |
| Leader profile | Works · Organizations · **Connected voices** · Themes |
| Articles / courses | Inline "in conversation with" citing foreword/co-teach edges |
| Footnotes / citations registry | EEAT: link to leader profile + primary source URL |
| Dashboard (tenant) | Private "network context" for onboarding, not public recruiting |

### SEO / GEO note
Collective authority compounds when **each leader's profile** cites the same movement vocabulary (missional, multiplication, microchurch, DMM) with **distinct** primary frameworks—avoid duplicate thin bios.

---

## 10. The scenius story in one paragraph (for copywriters)

These leaders are not a marketing coalition. They are a **pre-existing confederation of trainers, authors, and network directors** who have already co-written books, shared stages, endorsed one another's manuscripts, and trained the same church planters from Kansas City to Tampa to Chicago to the Front Range—while their **best material still lives in silos** (church sites, LMS instances, Amazon, Substack, conference CD-ROM legacies, and half-finished cohort PDFs). Movemental names them as **trusted voices** because their collective experience is the proof that mission-driven organizations deserve infrastructure that preserves voice, links related work, and makes formation inspectable—starting with the people who already practice scenius in the room, not only on paper.

---

## 11. Fragmentation → platform fit (operator view)

| Leader | Fragmentation signal (from corpus) | Platform-high-leverage move |
|--------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| Hirsch | Books + 4 orgs + seminaries | Unified hub; sermon/talk repurposing |
| Ferguson | Personal site + CCC + NewThing + Exponential | Sermon→article pipeline; series around Hero Maker / BLESS |
| Brisco | Articles across New Churches / ChurchLeaders + teaching backlog | Teaching→course; aggregate hub |
| Woodward | V3 + Missio + 3 seminaries + IVP/100M | Book→course series; cross-link academic/practical |
| Cooper | 17 books, 227 podcasts, ~zero owned audience | **Consolidate + index** (archetype onboarding win) |
| Sanders | Strong books; network content distributed | Microchurch resource graph |
| Wegner | Bridge content; Exponential role under-documented | Map Exponential edges; KC cluster cross-links |
| Rios | 3 domains, 2 newsletters, bilingual video | Passion2Plant/Púlpito as tenant POC |
| Moran | Podcast active; course layer thin | Hybrid Church learning path |
| Rowland | Thin personal site; rich org roles | *Red Skies* → evergreen series engine |
| The Chambers | Joint corpus split across V3 / Renovaré / Missio / Forge; two-person output under three folders | Unify as one dyad voice; practice library + Forge / Arise curriculum tie-in |

## 12. Research gaps before a public "network" ship

1. **Graph completeness:** Rob Wegner ↔ Exponential leadership stack; Brisco ↔ Wegner KC adjacency; Sanders ↔ Underground sister cities—edges noted in network summaries but not fully committed.
2. **Chambers dyad consolidation:** Collapse `jeremy-chambers`, `monica-chambers`, and `jeremy-monica-chambers` into one node (canonical `jeremy-monica-chambers`) everywhere it ships—roster, counts, graph, `/voices`. Retire the per-person folders from public surfaces.
3. **Off-cohort nodes:** Michael Frost, Lance Ford, Matt Smay, Jon Ferguson—high weight in story, not onboarded folders.
4. **Bree Mills:** Hold or remove from public counts until identity verified.
5. **Consent & publicity tier:** Which edges may appear on **public** `/voices` vs. dashboard-only.
6. **Visualization ethics:** Field-map doc warns against mixing real names for non-onboarded leaders in product viz—**Option A** (only evidenced onboarded nodes) remains the trust-safe default.
